---
title: "GSA_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA"
author: "Dr. Chih-Yu Hung"
date: "2026-04-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
# --- Packages ----
library(tidyverse)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{r data_and_function}
# --- Data preparation ----
# Goal: global sensitivity analysis of indirect N2O from volatilised biosolid N.
# The analysis evaluates the contribution of uncertain parameters to national CO2e variation.

target_year <- 2015

DF <- readRDS("Inputs/BiosolidN_corrected.rds") # Data from Chang's work

Data <- DF %>%
  filter(Inv_Year_ID == target_year) %>%
  select(
    Inv_Year_ID,
    Province_ID,
    Ecodistrict_ID,
    Fert_LCA_kg = BION_corrected
  ) %>%
  filter(Fert_LCA_kg > 0) %>%
  drop_na()


# --- Volatilisation N2O calculation ----
# The sampled inputs are applied as national multipliers or national parameter values.
# This keeps the GSA focused on key national drivers instead of row level random noise.


calc_vol_gsa <- function(df, Fert_scale, FRRC_GASM, EFVD) {
  df %>%
    mutate(
      Fert_LCA_kg = Fert_LCA_kg * Fert_scale,
      FRRC_GASM = FRRC_GASM,
      EFVD = EFVD,
      N_vol = Fert_LCA_kg * FRRC_GASM,
      N2O = N_vol * EFVD
    ) %>%
    summarise(
      Tot.Fert = sum(Fert_LCA_kg, na.rm = TRUE),
      Tot.Nvol = sum(N_vol, na.rm = TRUE),
      N2O = sum(N2O, na.rm = TRUE),
      N2O.IEF = if_else(Tot.Fert > 0, N2O / Tot.Fert, NA_real_),
      CO2e = N2O * 44 / 28 * 265,
      `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e / 10^6,
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}
```

```{r gsa_settings}
# --- User knobs ----
# n_gsa can be increased after testing the script with a smaller number.

n_gsa <- 5000
seed  <- 2026
set.seed(seed)

# --- Random draw helpers ----
# p95 is the fractional half width of the 95 percent confidence interval.
# A national fertilizer multiplier is used so all activity data move together in the GSA.

rnorm_ci <- function(mu, p95) {
  sd <- abs(p95) * abs(mu) / 1.96
  rnorm(length(mu), mean = mu, sd = sd)
}

rtri_q <- function(q, a, c, b) {
  stopifnot(a <= c, c <= b, a < b)
  Fc <- (c - a) / (b - a)
  x <- ifelse(
    q < Fc,
    a + sqrt(q * (b - a) * (c - a)),
    b - sqrt((1 - q) * (b - a) * (b - c))
  )
  pmax(a, pmin(x, b))
}

make_lhs <- function(n, k) {
  replicate(k, sample((seq_len(n) - runif(n)) / n)) %>%
    as_tibble(.name_repair = "minimal")
}
```

```{r lhs_design}
# --- Latin hypercube design ----
# Inputs included in the GSA:
# Fert_LCA_kg represents national biosolid N activity data.
# FRRC_GASM represents the volatilisation fraction.
# EFVD represents the indirect N2O emission factor for volatilised N.

Tot_Fert <- sum(Data$Fert_LCA_kg, na.rm = TRUE)

U <- make_lhs(n_gsa, 3)
names(U) <- c("u_fert", "u_frrc", "u_efvd")

GSA_design <- U %>%
  transmute(
    GSA = row_number(),
    Fert_scale = pmax(qnorm(u_fert, mean = 1, sd = sqrt(0.15^2 + 0.46^2) / 1.96), 0),
    Fert_LCA_kg = Tot_Fert * Fert_scale,
    FRRC_GASM = pmin(pmax(qnorm(u_frrc, mean = 0.20, sd = 0.20 * 0.20 / 1.96), 0), 1),
    EFVD = rtri_q(u_efvd, a = 0.002, c = 0.010, b = 0.040)
  )

summary(GSA_design) #Fert_scale 1.00003, 0.99999, Fert_LCA_kg 16036122, 16035463
```

```{r run_gsa}
# --- Run GSA simulations ----
# One national emission result is calculated for each parameter set.

Result_GSA <- pmap_dfr(
  GSA_design,
  function(GSA, Fert_scale, Fert_LCA_kg, FRRC_GASM, EFVD) {
    calc_vol_gsa(
      Data,
      Fert_scale = Fert_scale,
      FRRC_GASM = FRRC_GASM,
      EFVD = EFVD
    ) %>%
      mutate(
        GSA = GSA,
        Fert_scale = Fert_scale,
        Fert_LCA_kg = Fert_LCA_kg,
        FRRC_GASM = FRRC_GASM,
        EFVD = EFVD
      )
  }
) %>%
  select(GSA, Fert_scale, Fert_LCA_kg, FRRC_GASM, EFVD, everything())

summary(Result_GSA$`CO2e(kt)`) #  20.602, 23.146
saveRDS(Result_GSA, "Outputs/GSA_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.rds")

```

```{r prcc_function}
# --- Partial rank correlation coefficient function ----
# PRCC measures the strength of the monotonic relationship between each input and the output,
# after removing the rank effects of the other inputs.

calc_prcc <- function(df, y_col, x_cols) {
  dat <- df %>%
    select(all_of(c(y_col, x_cols))) %>%
    drop_na() %>%
    mutate(across(everything(), rank))
  
  map_dfr(x_cols, function(x) {
    z <- setdiff(x_cols, x)
    ry <- if (length(z) == 0) dat[[y_col]] else resid(lm(reformulate(z, y_col), data = dat))
    rx <- if (length(z) == 0) dat[[x]]     else resid(lm(reformulate(z, x),     data = dat))
    ct <- cor.test(rx, ry, method = "pearson")
    
    tibble(
      input = x,
      PRCC = unname(ct$estimate),
      p_value = ct$p.value
    )
  }) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(PRCC)))
}
```

```{r prcc_result}
# --- Calculate PRCC ----
# Positive PRCC means the output increases as the input increases.
# Larger absolute PRCC means a stronger monotonic influence on national emissions.

x_cols <- c("Fert_LCA_kg", "FRRC_GASM", "EFVD")

Result_GSA$CO2e_kt <- Result_GSA$`CO2e(kt)`

prcc_tbl <- calc_prcc(Result_GSA, "CO2e_kt", x_cols)

prcc_tbl

write.csv(
  prcc_tbl,
  "Outputs/GSA_PRCC_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```

```{r contribution_result}
# --- Relative contribution based on PRCC ----
# This is a practical contribution metric based on normalized squared PRCC.
# It should be reported as sensitivity contribution, not as emission share.

contribution_tbl <- prcc_tbl %>%
  mutate(
    abs_PRCC = abs(PRCC),
    contribution = PRCC^2 / sum(PRCC^2) * 100
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(contribution))

contribution_tbl # EFVD 44%, Biosolid N 38%, Frac_GASM 18%
write.csv(contribution_tbl, "Outputs/GSA_Contribution_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r plot_prcc}
# --- Plot PRCC result ----
# The plot ranks the key uncertain inputs by their monotonic effect on national emissions.

p1 <- ggplot(prcc_tbl, aes(x = reorder(input, abs(PRCC)), y = PRCC)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "PRCC",
    title = "Global sensitivity of indirect volatilisation N2O emissions"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p1

ggsave(
  "Outputs/GSA_PRCC_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.png",
  p1,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```

```{r plot_scatter}
# --- Scatter plot for input output relationships ----
# This figure helps check whether relationships are monotonic, which supports PRCC interpretation.

p2 <- Result_GSA %>%
  pivot_longer(all_of(x_cols), names_to = "input", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value, y = `CO2e(kt)`)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ input, scales = "free_x") +
  labs(
    x = "Input value",
    y = expression(N[2]*O~CO[2]*e~"(kt)"),
    title = "Input output relationships for indirect volatilisation N2O emissions"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p2

ggsave(
  "Outputs/GSA_Scatter_Indirect_Volatilisation_Biosolid_CA.png",
  p2,
  width = 8,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```
